title: 10 best herbal teas for sunburn

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce redness and swelling caused by sunburn, promoting faster healing and relief from discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in aloe vera tea, including vitamins C and E, help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and accelerate the aging process, reducing the appearance of sun-damaged skin.

Sothing and moisturizing properties: Aloe vera's gel and other compounds have a soothing and moisturizing effect on the skin, helping to calm irritation, reduce itchiness, and promote hydration, which aids in speeding up the healing process.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, teas helps with sunburn because of its soothing and cooling properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that work together to calm and hydrate sunburned skin. Drinking aloe vera tea can help to reduce inflammation and promote the healing process. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in the tea can also help to ease the discomfort and pain associated with sunburn, making it a popular natural remedy for sunburn relief.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a lignan that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
Catechins: Catechins are a type of phenolic compound that have antioxidant properties, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting the healing process of sunburn.
Aloin: Aloin is a anthraquinone glycoside that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, helping to calm and moisturize sunburned skin, reducing the risk of blistering and peeling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high gel content, which provides soothing and cooling effects to sunburned skin.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their antioxidant properties, which help to protect the skin from further damage and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-12 ounces of fresh aloe vera gel and 1 cup of water for brewing.
Combine aloe vera gel with water in a saucepan and bring to a boil over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes allowing the flavors to meld together.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl to remove solids.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional 5-10 minutes before serving over ice or chilled.

remedy_properties: Emollient property: Marshmallow tea's emollient property helps to soothe and calm sunburned skin by forming a protective barrier that locks in moisture, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory property: Marshmallow tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as mucilage and flavonoids, help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn, promoting faster healing and relief.

Soothing property: The soothing properties of marshmallow tea, particularly its mucilage content, help to calm irritated skin and reduce itching, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving the discomfort of sunburn.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, teas helps with sunburn because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The root of the marshmallow plant contains mucilages, which form a protective barrier on the skin's surface, reducing heat and discomfort from sunburn. This natural shield also helps to retain moisture, promoting the skin's natural healing process and reducing the risk of peeling and blistering. As a result, Althaea officinalis teas can provide relief and aid in the recovery from sunburn.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: These complex carbohydrates help soothe and protect sunburned skin by forming a protective barrier, reducing inflammation, and promoting healing.

Phenolic acids (such as gallic acid and ellagic acid): These antioxidants neutralize free radicals, reduce oxidative stress, and inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to sunburn pain and inflammation.

Flavonoids (such as quercetin): These antioxidants help mitigate sunburn damage by reducing inflammation, protecting against UV-induced DNA damage, and promoting collagen synthesis to support skin repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high mucilage content, which provides soothing and protective properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make teas, as they contain mucilage and other compounds that help to calm and moisturize sunburned skin.
Stems: The stems are sometimes used, although less commonly than roots or leaves, to make teas that can provide similar soothing and protective effects.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried althaea officinalis root from a trusted supplier.
Heat 1 cup of boiling water for 2-3 minutes in a teapot.
Add the gathered root to the heated water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Consume the althaea officinalis tea immediately or store it in the refrigerator for later use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Pot marigold tea contains flavonoids like luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness and swelling caused by sunburn.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, can help neutralize free radicals generated by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and damage to the skin.
Soften and soothe property: Pot marigold tea contains saponins and mucilages that have been shown to soften and soothe irritated skin, making it an effective treatment for sunburn relief.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, teas helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids and carotenoids present in Calendula help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with sunburn. Its antiseptic properties also help prevent infection and promote wound healing, allowing the skin to recover faster from sun damage. As a natural remedy, Calendula tea provides relief and comfort to sunburned skin, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of further damage.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol have antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation, promoting healing and reducing the risk of sunburn.
N-alkanoyl-4-hydroxyphenylalanine amide (HPPA): This compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties, which can aid in the recovery of sunburned skin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds.
Leaves: They contain similar properties to flowers, making them a suitable alternative for soothing sunburned skin.
Stems: The stems of Calendula officinalis also contain anti-inflammatory properties, which can be beneficial in reducing redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried calendula officinalis flowers in late afternoon for optimal potency retention.
Rinse the dried flowers gently with lukewarm water to remove any impurities or debris immediately.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried calendula officinalis flowers with 1 cup of boiling water to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids completely.
Drink the tea immediately while warm to reap the full benefits of calendula officinalis.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort tea contains antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by sunburn, promoting faster healing and reducing redness.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe sunburned skin and reduce pain, swelling, and discomfort.
Wound-healing properties: St John's Wort has been shown to stimulate collagen synthesis and improve wound healing, which can help promote the natural repair process of sunburned skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, teas helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the plant have been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn. Additionally, the tea's ability to soothe and calm the skin can help to reduce the risk of infection and promote faster healing. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to treat various skin conditions, making it a popular choice for sunburn relief.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin is a phenolic compound found in St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum) that helps with sunburn by inhibiting inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin, thereby reducing redness and pain associated with sunburn.

Naphthodianthrones: Naphthodianthrones, including hypericin and pseudohypericin, are phytochemicals present in St. John's Wort that help with sunburn by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the severity of sunburn symptoms.

Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid found in Hypericum perforatum that helps with sunburn by acting as a potent antioxidant, neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress in the skin, which can contribute to the development of sunburn and skin damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids, specifically quercetin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe sunburned skin.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, they provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects to reduce sunburn discomfort.
Stems: They contain sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help calm sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 teaspoons of fresh or 3-4 teaspoons of dried hypericum perforatum flowers at dawn when dew is present.
Steep the harvested hypericum perforatum in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to release.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup to remove the solids.
Discard the solids and add honey or lemon to taste if desired to enhance the flavor.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before serving and consuming it immediately.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing skin damage and inflammation associated with sunburn.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with sunburned skin.

Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenoids have a direct soothing effect on the skin, providing quick relief from burning sensations, discomfort, and skin irritation caused by sunburn.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, teas helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The tea's flavonoids and terpenoids work to calm redness and itching, reducing the discomfort associated with sunburn. Additionally, chamomile's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, promoting skin repair and speeding up the healing process. As a result, Matricaria chamomilla teas have become a popular natural remedy for soothing sunburned skin and promoting a more comfortable recovery.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid compound that helps with sunburn by acting as an antioxidant, reducing inflammation and protecting skin cells from damage caused by UV radiation.
Apolligenin: A flavonoid compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing redness and discomfort.

Matricaria chamomilla triterpene glycosides: A group of compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and soothing properties, helping to calm and protect sunburned skin, reducing inflammation and promoting skin repair.
remedy_parts: Flowers: These are the primary source for making teas to alleviate sunburn due to their high concentration of apigenin and other anti-inflammatory compounds.
Leaves: The leaves of Matricaria chamomilla contain flavonoids and other compounds that help soothe and calm sunburned skin when made into a tea.
Seeds: Matricaria chamomilla seeds are sometimes used to make teas for sunburn relief, as they possess anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in healing and reducing discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers or 2-3 tablespoons of dried flowers at dawn when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm dark place for 1-2 weeks to maintain their medicinal properties.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to allow their active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the flowers and enjoy your herbal matricaria chamomilla tea.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburned skin.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in licorice tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and promoting skin healing.

Sothing and moisturizing properties: Licorice root contains mucilage, a natural thickening agent that provides a soothing and protective barrier on the skin, helping to calm and hydrate sunburned areas.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The compounds found in Glycyrrhiza glabra, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing. As a result, the tea can help to soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, its antioxidant properties can help to protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation, promoting a faster recovery from sunburn.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help protect the skin from damage caused by UV radiation and inflammation, reducing the severity of sunburn symptoms.
Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe sunburned skin, reduce redness, and promote healing.
Liquiritin: A glycoside present in Glycyrrhiza glabra, liquiritin is known for its antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress caused by sun exposure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make teas for sunburn relief due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make teas for sunburn relief due to their cooling and astringent properties, which help reduce redness and itching.
(Optional) Stems: The stems of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make teas for sunburn relief due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in skin recovery.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra from a reputable supplier in quantities of 2 to 3 grams per serving.
Rinse the dried roots with cold water to remove any impurities or debris from the packaging.
Boil 1 liter of water in a pot for 5 to 7 minutes to create a rolling boil.
Steep the dried roots in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids to serve the herbal tea hot.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Peppermint tea's antioxidant compounds, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in sunburned skin.

Anti-inflammatory Effects: The menthol content in peppermint tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn, while also soothing the skin and promoting healing.

Cooling and Soothing Properties: Peppermint tea's cooling sensation helps to calm and relax the skin, reducing heat and discomfort caused by sunburn, while also providing a comforting and calming effect on the body.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and cooling properties. The menthol in peppermint tea soothes sunburned skin, reducing redness and swelling. The antioxidants present in the tea also help to neutralize free radicals, which can cause further damage to the skin. Additionally, peppermint tea has a calming effect, which can help to reduce the discomfort and pain associated with sunburn, providing a sense of relief and comfort to affected areas.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol has a cooling effect that helps to reduce heat and discomfort associated with sunburn, providing temporary relief.
Rosmarinic acid (phenolic diterpenoid): Rosmarinic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain caused by sunburn.

Caryophyllene oxide (sesquiterpenoid): Caryophyllene oxide has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and cell damage caused by UV radiation, thereby accelerating the healing process of sunburn.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for sunburn relief due to their high content of menthol, which helps to soothe and cool the skin.
Stems: Used due to their ability to provide a calming effect and reduce inflammation, making them a potential remedy for sunburn relief.
Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves by pinching or cutting off stems just above the node to encourage new growth.
Dry the leaves in a single layer by spreading them out on a paper bag or tray.
Infuse dried leaves in a cup by steeping 1 teaspoon in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea by pouring it through a fine-mesh sieve to remove the leaves and sediment.
Serve the tea hot or iced and enjoy its refreshing and cooling properties immediately.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as arctiin and luteolin, help reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn, promoting faster healing.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in burdock tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting natural regeneration.

Cooling and soothing properties: Burdock tea's cooling and soothing effects, attributed to its mucilaginous compounds like inulin and fructooligosaccharides, help calm irritated sunburned skin, reducing discomfort and itching.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arctium lappa, also known as burdock, teas helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties and rich antioxidant content. The plant's roots contain arctiin, a compound that has been shown to soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing redness and discomfort. The tea's cooling effect helps to reduce heat and inflammation, promoting faster healing of the skin. Additionally, burdock's high water content and astringent properties make it an effective natural remedy for sunburn relief.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin is a type of fructan that can help soothe and protect the skin from further damage caused by sunburn, reducing inflammation and promoting skin repair.
Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides, particularly those with antioxidant properties, can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress caused by UV radiation, which can exacerbate sunburn.

Lignans: Lignans in Arctium lappa may help reduce inflammation and alleviate sunburn symptoms by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Arctium lappa are used to make teas for sunburn because of their anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots are used to make teas for sunburn due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.
Seeds: Seeds are used to make teas for sunburn because they contain compounds that help soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried arctium lappa root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the liquid using a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Discard the solids and store the herbal tea in a covered container in the refrigerator.
Serve the cooled tea chilled or at room temperature as desired within 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Dandelion tea's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with sunburn.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids in dandelion tea may help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.

Cooling and soothing properties: Dandelion's natural cooling properties, such as its ability to stimulate the production of prostaglandins, can help soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, teas helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling. The tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant effects, which help to neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation. Additionally, the tea's soothing properties can calm the skin, providing relief from the discomfort and pain associated with sunburn. This natural remedy can also aid in the healing process, promoting a faster recovery from sunburn.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by UV radiation, thereby alleviating sunburn symptoms.
Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe sunburned skin and promote healing.
Coumarins: These natural compounds possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by UV radiation and reduce the risk of sunburn.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties to help ease sunburn discomfort.
Flowers: Utilized for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties to reduce redness and promote skin recovery.
Roots: Employed for their cooling and anti-inflammatory effects to help calm sunburned skin and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the dandelion roots and leaves in early morning to ensure optimal freshness and flavor.
Rinse 2-3 teaspoons of dandelion roots and 2-3 teaspoons of dandelion leaves in cold water for 5 minutes.
Combine the rinsed dandelion roots and leaves with 1 quart of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl for 5 minutes.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature and serve immediately or refrigerate for up to 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains potent anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with sunburns by inhibiting prostaglandins' inflammatory effects.
Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant activity helps neutralize free radicals generated from UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting recovery from sunburn.

Soothing and calming compounds: The volatile oils in ginger, such as citral and geraniol, have a soothing and calming effect on the skin, which can help relieve itching, redness, and discomfort caused by sunburn.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, teas helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds present in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with sunburn. Drinking ginger tea may help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help with sunburn by reducing inflammation and pain due to their potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Shogaols: Assist in alleviating sunburn symptoms by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing oxidative stress.
Zingiberene: Contribute to the relief of sunburn by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help to soothe and calm damaged skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Ginger roots are also used to make teas for sunburn, as they contain compounds that help to reduce pain and inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves of the ginger plant are sometimes used to make teas for sunburn, as they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried zingiber officinale root and 1 cup of boiling water for the infusion.
Steep the dried zingiber officinale root in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the infused liquid through a fine mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Add honey or other sweetener to taste and stir well to combine with the tea.
Serve the zingiber officinale tea immediately and enjoy its warm spicy flavor and potential health benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with sunburn because of its soothing and calming properties. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as luteolin and apigenin, help to reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn. Additionally, the tea's cooling effect can provide instant relief from discomfort and itching. As the body heals, English lavender teas promote hydration, which is essential for replenishing lost moisture in burned skin. By drinking English lavender tea, individuals can enjoy a calming and soothing experience that aids in their recovery from sunburn.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress caused by UV radiation, which can contribute to sunburn and skin damage.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing redness and discomfort.
Buds: The buds contain flavonoids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in relieving sunburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers or 3-4 teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Combine the herbs with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the volatile oils to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before applying it topically or consuming it internally.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder teas helps with sunburn because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the skin. The tea's antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to neutralize free radicals caused by UV damage, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, elder tea's cooling effects help to ease discomfort and itching associated with sunburn. Soothing and cooling effect: Elder tea has a calming and cooling effect on the skin, which can help ease the heat, itching, and stinging sensations associated with sunburn.

Barks: The barks of the elder plant contain salicin, similar to aspirin, which helps reduce pain and inflammation associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves.
Steep the sambucus nigra in 8 ounces of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to sweeten the tea and stir well.
Allow the tea to cool for at least 30 minutes before drinking.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover teas helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tea's flavonoids, particularly triterpenes, help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with sunburn, while its antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells. Additionally, the tea's astringent properties can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Vasodilatory compounds: Red clover tea contains isoflavones that can help dilate blood vessels, increasing blood flow to the affected area and promoting healing and tissue repair after sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which help soothe and calm irritated skin, making them effective for treating sunburn.
Flowers: Contain apigenin, a flavonoid that helps reduce inflammation and promote healing, making them beneficial for calming sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried trifolium pratense flowers measuring one tablespoon for every eight ounces of water.
Rinse the harvested flowers in cold running water to remove any dirt or debris and pat dry.
Steep the rinsed flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes allowing their natural compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large bowl discarding the solids.
Allow the strained tea to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it and drinking up to three times daily.

Barks: Sage barks contain tannins, which help soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing irritation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried salvia officinalis leaves measuring one teaspoon per eight ounces water.
Combine the dried herbs in a teapot or infuser with boiling water for five minutes steeping time.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove loose particles.
Let the tea cool to room temperature before applying it topically as a compress or bath.

remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing sunburn pain and inflammation.
Buds: Thyme buds contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to protect the skin from further damage caused by UV rays and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves from your garden or store dried thyme in an airtight container for use.
Measure one teaspoon of dried thyme or three teaspoons of fresh thyme per cup of boiling water.
Steep the thyme mixture in hot water for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool for several minutes before consuming.
Drink one to two cups of thymus vulgaris tea every hour or as needed to alleviate sunburn symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with sunburn because of its unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory properties, and soothing compounds. The tea's rich flavonoids and polyphenols work to reduce inflammation and alleviate redness and discomfort associated with sunburn. Additionally, the tea's natural astringent properties help to tighten and calm the skin, providing relief from itching and stinging sensations. As a result, drinking Tasmanian blue gum teas can provide rapid and effective relief for sunburned skin, helping to promote healing and reduce scarring.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum tea contains powerful antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing skin damage and inflammation associated with sunburn.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea has been shown to have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with sunburn, providing relief and speeding up the healing process.

Cooling and soothing properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum tea has a calming effect on the skin, making it an excellent natural remedy for cooling and soothing sunburned skin, reducing itching and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Bark: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling caused by sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves from a reputable source.
Measure out one teaspoon of the dried eucalyptus leaves into a tea infuser or strainer.
Bring 8 ounces of boiling water to a simmer and steep the eucalyptus leaves for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser or strainer, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before applying it topically as a compress to soothe sunburned skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with sunburn because it contains flavonoids, which possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The tea's astringent properties also work to cool and soothe the skin, reducing redness and irritation. Additionally, yarrow's natural antiseptic properties can help prevent infection and promote healthy wound healing. As a result, drinking yarrow tea after a sunburn can provide relief from heat, itching, and discomfort, allowing the skin to recover more quickly and effectively.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like chamazulene and bisabolol, which help reduce inflammation and discomfort caused by sunburn.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in yarrow tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and worsen sunburn symptoms.

Vasodilatory effects: Yarrow's vasodilatory properties help to dilate blood vessels, which can aid in reducing heat and discomfort associated with sunburn by promoting blood flow and removing excess heat from the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming sunburned skin.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds contain flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making them a valuable addition to medicinal teas for sunburn relief.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried achillea millefolium flowers per cup of boiling water.
Combine the flowers and water in a teapot or infuser for five minutes of steeping time.
Strain the tea into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove solids.
Allow the tea to cool for two minutes before drinking it slowly over the next hour.
Repeat the process twice daily for three days to help soothe sunburned skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with sunburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the skin. As the tea is absorbed into the skin, it helps promote healing and reduces the appearance of peeling and flaking.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Plantain tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which help reduce swelling and redness caused by sunburn.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in plantain tea, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals and prevent further damage to the skin from UV radiation.

Wound-healing properties: Plantain tea has been shown to promote wound healing and tissue repair by increasing collagen synthesis, improving blood flow, and reducing bacterial growth, which can help accelerate the recovery process for sunburned skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, helping to reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
Rhyzomes: Containing soothing agents that calm irritated skin and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried plantago major leaves from a trusted source.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of the dried herb into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour in 1 cup of boiling water over the herb and let it steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a clean cup and discard the solids after 10-15 minutes.
Enjoy the soothing tea immediately, allowing its calming effects to take hold.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to sunburn that are treated with herbal teas are skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and rosacea. These conditions often cause redness, inflammation, and discomfort, similar to sunburn. Herbal teas like chamomile, calendula, and peppermint have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching. Additionally, herbal teas like green tea and turmeric have antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from further damage and promote healing.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for sunburn are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the discomfort and pain caused by excessive sun exposure. These teas are made from various herbs that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which work to calm and repair sun-damaged skin. The best herbal teas for sunburn include Calendula officinalis, Hypericum perforatum, Aloe barbadensis, Aloe vera, Arnica montana, Symphytum officinale, Plantago lanceolata, Ginkgo biloba, Urtica dioica, and Glycyrrhiza glabra. If left untreated, sunburn can lead to severe discomfort, increased risk of skin cancer, and long-term damage to the skin, impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with sunburn is a soothing blend of cooling peppermint, calming chamomile, and hydrating aloe vera. Peppermint tea helps to reduce inflammation and cool the skin, while chamomile tea provides relief from itching and irritation. Aloe vera tea promotes skin regeneration and hydration, aiding in the healing process. Drinking this combination can also help to reduce redness and discomfort associated with sunburn, promoting a faster recovery and soothing relief.
